title: jacobaea paludosa subsp. lanata
intro: Jacobaea paludosa subsp. lanata, also known as marsh marsh marigold or woolly ragwort, is a rare plant species belonging to the dandelion family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other compounds. The health benefits of Jacobaea paludosa subsp. lanata include the potential to treat various conditions such as skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, tinctures, or as a topical cream, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
